creator: mat@FreeBSD.org
text:
No.  There is no timeout for portmgr.  The Porter's Handbook clearly states:

  Exceptions to this are anything maintained by the
  Ports Management Team <portmgr@FreeBSD.org>, or the
  Security Officer Team <security-officer@FreeBSD.org>.
  No unauthorized commits may ever be made to ports
  maintained by those groups.

Also, I am not sure agree with the "and documentation" bit for _MAN, man pages are installed with 444 mode.  It would change how the documentation is installed, and as it often contains config files, users could end up with a non-editable file.

Also, the comments at the top of bsd.*.mk are documenting the code, they are *not* for general consumption.  It does not really matter if they're not exhaustives.  The Porter's Handbook is here for that.
